text: What if we could sniff the air of distant planets? When an exoplanet crosses its star, starlight filters through its atmosphere. This process, called transmission spectroscopy, helps us detect molecules like water and methane. Each molecule absorbs specific wavelengths, acting like a cosmic fingerprint. This groundbreaking method could reveal the potential for life beyond Earth. Curious about what else we can discover in the cosmos? Hit like and follow for more insights!
